deploy_target.note: One task: generate_omop_data writes the 6 OMOP tables (deterministic seed=42, safe to re-run). This is the data foundation the ML build reads read-only. Deploy from a web terminal, never from inside Genie Code. The job runs the generator once and completes.

toggle.note: yes = run the generator into client_catalog.client_schema (synthetic, no PHI). no = the generate_omop_data task NO-OPS (the job passes run_with_synthetic_data as the 3rd script param); the ML build then reads the 6 OMOP tables from source_catalog.source_schema (your own OMOP source). The ML kit must ALSO be repointed: it sets its own source_catalog/source_schema; setting it here alone does not repoint it. The 6 OMOP table NAMES are identical in both modes, so no table-name changes are needed, but a few synthetic-only COLUMNS are absent from a standard OMOP source (see synthetic_only_columns) and must be stripped from downstream code in real mode.

synthetic_only_columns.purpose: High-profile / VIP flag for the Governance row-filter / ABAC demo. A fixed set of 6 person_ids (3, 12, 37, 44, 88, 205) are flagged true, deliberately overlapping BOTH trial-eligible cohorts (Trial A 1-20, Trial B 31-50) so a row-level policy visibly removes eligible patients from the pre-screen.
synthetic_only_columns.real_data_behavior: SYNTHETIC ONLY. A standard OMOP source does not have this column, so downstream references must be dropped or repointed in real mode. In real mode the generator no-ops, so this column does not exist. Any downstream code that references person.is_high_profile (a Governance row filter or mask, a SELECT list, a join, a WHERE clause) MUST be dropped or guarded when switching to real data, or the query fails with UNRESOLVED_COLUMN. If your OMOP source has a high-profile / VIP indicator, repoint the row filter at that column instead of deleting the logic.

tables.name: person
tables.role: One row per patient. Key columns: person_id (PK), year_of_birth, gender_source_value, birth_date. Also carries is_high_profile (boolean), a SYNTHETIC-ONLY VIP flag (see synthetic_only_columns); a standard OMOP source does NOT have it.

tables.name: condition_occurrence
tables.role: Breast cancer diagnoses. condition_source_value = 'Malignant neoplasm of breast' identifies breast cancer patients.

tables.name: measurement
tables.role: Structured biomarker results (ER/PR/HER2). measurement_source_value identifies the marker; value_source_value = 'Positive'/'Negative'. Absent for notes-only patients (person_ids 181-240).

tables.name: observation
tables.role: Menopausal status (observation_source_value='Menopausal status') and AJCC tumor stage (observation_source_value='AJCC stage'). Each patient gets both observation types.

tables.name: drug_exposure
tables.role: Prior therapy. drug_source_value='Trastuzumab' or 'Pertuzumab' flags prior anti-HER2 therapy, a disqualifier for Trial A (person_ids 21-30 are ineligible controls).

tables.name: note
tables.role: Free-text pathology reports in note_text. Contains biomarker phrasing for both-agree patients (1-180) and notes-only patients (181-240); no biomarker phrasing for structured-only patients (241-300). The NLP value story: ~60 patients (181-240) are invisible to structured queries but discoverable via note_text extraction.

trial_a.name: HER2-Positive Breast Cancer
trial_a.ineligible_reason: Prior anti-HER2 therapy (Trastuzumab and/or Pertuzumab in drug_exposure)

both_agree.description: Structured AND note biomarkers present. NLP ground truth for MLflow evaluation. Includes the hard_cases sub-band below.

notes_only.description: Biomarker status in note_text ONLY, invisible to structured queries. The NLP value story. Notes are written clearly so recovery is reliable.

structured_only.description: Structured measurement rows only; note_text has no biomarker phrasing.

hard_cases.description: Sub-band of both_agree. Structured value is the definite gold label, but the pathology note is written equivocally (HER2 IHC 2+ with a reflex FISH ratio and no stated conclusion; ER-low-positive 1-8%; subtype line withheld). Purpose: give the MLflow / GenAI evaluation genuine contrast (a careful prompt or stronger model resolves these; a terse prompt slips to Unknown). Does NOT change any eligibility count because the gold layer takes the structured value. Controlled by HARD_CASE_IDS in generate_omop_data.py.

gold.note: The foundation has no SDP pipeline. All 6 OMOP tables are written directly by the generator and behave as analytics-ready OMOP CDM tables. Downstream kits build bronze/silver/gold on top.

dependency_map.note: ML runs ai_query NLP extraction over note_text

grain_constraints.note: One row per patient, 300 rows total
Up to 3 rows per patient (ER, PR, HER2); absent for notes-only group (181-240)
2 rows per patient: menopausal status + AJCC stage

source_inputs.description: Faker-based Python script. generate_omop_data.py writes all 6 OMOP tables (argv[1]=catalog, argv[2]=schema, seed=42 for determinism).

Your own OMOP CDM tables. Because both the synthetic tables and your source follow the OMOP Common Data Model (a public open standard), the same queries run unchanged EXCEPT for the synthetic-only demo columns listed in synthetic_only_columns (e.g. person.is_high_profile), which do not exist in a standard OMOP source; strip downstream references to those in real mode.

verify_queries.description: Row counts for all 6 OMOP tables, all must be > 0 after the generate_omop_data task
verify_queries.sql: SELECT 'person' AS tbl, COUNT(*) AS n FROM ${client_catalog}.${client_schema}.person UNION ALL SELECT 'condition_occurrence', COUNT(*) FROM ${client_catalog}.${client_schema}.condition_occurrence UNION ALL SELECT 'measurement', COUNT(*) FROM ${client_catalog}.${client_schema}.measurement UNION ALL SELECT 'observation', COUNT(*) FROM ${client_catalog}.${client_schema}.observation UNION ALL SELECT 'drug_exposure', COUNT(*) FROM ${client_catalog}.${client_schema}.drug_exposure UNION ALL SELECT 'note', COUNT(*) FROM ${client_catalog}.${client_schema}.note ORDER BY tbl
verify_queries.expected_synthetic: person=300, condition_occurrence=300, measurement=720, observation=720, drug_exposure=383, note=265

verify_queries.description: Patients with pathology notes but no structured biomarker measurements (the NLP value story)
verify_queries.sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT n.person_id) AS n FROM ${client_catalog}.${client_schema}.note n WHERE n.person_id NOT IN (SELECT DISTINCT person_id FROM ${client_catalog}.${client_schema}.measurement WHERE measurement_source_value IN ('HER2/neu','Estrogen receptor','Progesterone receptor'))

lock_targets.reason: OMOP CDM table names (person, condition_occurrence, measurement, observation, drug_exposure, note) are standard. The synth/real toggle works because synthetic and real OMOP tables share identical names. Renaming breaks the toggle and the downstream kits.
Adaptation skill must not modify itself
